title: podman stop fails with "kill network process: permission denied" when stopping a rootless container that is in an --infra=false pod AND has a separate --network
body:
### Issue Description

Environment
Podman Version: 5.4.2
OS: Ubuntu 25.10 (Vanilla installation from official ISO)
Podman Build: Netavark-only (build does not support CNI/slirp4netns)
Network Backend: Netavark + Pasta
Mode: Rootless (as a regular user sadmin)
podman version output:

```
Client:        Podman Engine
Version:       5.4.2
API Version:   5.4.2
Go Version:    go1.24.4
Built:         Tue Jul  8 07:15:12 2025
Build Origin:  Ubuntu
OS/Arch:       linux/amd64
```

### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue

1. Ensure a clean state: podman system reset
2. Create a user-defined network: podman network create test-net
3. Create an infra-less pod: podman pod create --name test-pod-final --infra=false
4. Create a container that is both in the pod and attached to the network, with its own ports:
5. podman run -d --name test-nginx-final --pod test-pod-final --network test-net -p 8080:80 nginx:latest
6. Attempt to stop the container: podman stop --log-level debug test-nginx-final

Alternative Steps using podman-compose

1. Setup the compose.yml
```
version: '3.8'
services:
  nginx:
    image: nginx:latest
    container_name: nginx-container
    ports:
      - "8080:80"
      - "8443:443"
    restart: always
```
2. podman-compose up -d
3. podman-compose down

Output: 
```
Error: removing container 0f1a0ff527029c1bb726c5eaed3f09837b41bc4369e45b5810b0b2b915785ecc network: 1 error occurred:
	* rootless netns: kill network process: permission denied


nginx-container
985f705f3f7b3250662e81cc7d07ace2730c8e94db92ad64265b4649b85c0318
nginx_default
```


### Describe the results you received

Actual Behavior

The podman stop command fails with a "permission denied" error related to the rootless network namespace.

Error: removing container bafbb50166265e722dfaf8535060b9956bc995cecac664055f6daa783ebf2715 network: 1 error occurred:
	* rootless netns: kill network process: permission denied


Debug Logs

### Describe the results you expected

The container test-nginx-final stops cleanly without any errors.

### podman info output

### Podman in a container

No

### Privileged Or Rootless

Rootless

### Upstream Latest Release

No

### Additional environment details

_No response_

### Additional information

Additional Context & Diagnostics

1. This bug does not occur if the container is created standalone (e.g., podman run -d --name test -p 8080:80 nginx:latest). A standalone container stops cleanly.
2. This bug does not occur if the container is in a normal pod (with an infra container) and simply shares the pod's network (e.g., podman pod create --name test-pod -p 8080:80 followed by podman run -d --name test-nginx --pod test-pod nginx:latest). This container also stops cleanly.
3. The bug appears to be triggered only by the specific, complex combination of --pod, --infra=false, --network, and -p flags.
4. This specific flag combination is used by podman-compose (version 1.3.0) to emulate Docker Compose's behavior, which is how the bug was originally discovered.
5. The systemd user service (podman.service / podman.socket) is stopped and disabled. podman is run directly as a client.
6. User linger is enabled.
7. This is not a simple OS permission issue: the user can manually kill the pasta network helper process without error.

comments.body:
This doesn't seems like a upstream issues, something is blocking our access to kill  the pasta process, I guess apparmor so can you disable that and try again. IIRC there was a bug report about that @sbrivio-rh pinged me about somewhere but I cannot find it right now.

We don't ship apparmor profiles for podman here so this would have to be patched in whatever profile is being used for this.

As for the reproducer I think you just complicate things, this really has nothing to do with pods. I assume the trivial reproducer would be something like this to just use the bridge (custom networks) not pasta directly.

```
podman run --rm  --network bridge  quay.io/libpod/testimage:20241011 true
```

> This doesn't seems like a upstream issues, something is blocking our access to kill the pasta process, I guess apparmor so can you disable that and try again. IIRC there was a bug report about that [@sbrivio-rh](https://github.com/sbrivio-rh) pinged me about somewhere but I cannot find it right now.

I guess it was https://bugs.launchpad.net/ubuntu/+source/passt/+bug/2077158 but this is Ubuntu 25.10 so it should be solved there.

We had https://bugs.debian.org/cgi-bin/bugreport.cgi?bug=1100135 reported on Debian, but that should be fixed in Ubuntu too I suppose? @jackyhui I don't have a recent Ubuntu image running anywhere, could you please check?

> As for the reproducer I think you just complicate things, this really has nothing to do with pods. I assume the trivial reproducer would be something like this to just use the bridge (custom networks) not pasta directly.
> 
> ```
> podman run --rm  --network bridge  quay.io/libpod/testimage:20241011 true
> ```

...and, once you reproduce that the next time, it would be good to have the relevant lines from `/var/log/audit/audit.log`, in case there's any access denial caused by AppArmor.

> We had https://bugs.debian.org/cgi-bin/bugreport.cgi?bug=1100135 reported on Debian,

Ah yes that is the one I had in mind.

Same problem here after upgrading Kubuntu from 25.04 to 25.10, ~disabling apparmor temporary helps~:

```ini
# /etc/default/grub

GRUB_CMDLINE_LINUX_DEFAULT="... apparmor=0"
```

### Update

Seems apparmor is at least not the problem here. I've done following to solve it:
1. Stopped all containers
2. `podman system prune -a`
3. Label all mounted volumes with `:z` for shared & `:Z` for private, e.g. `ro,z` (may this solves problems with apparmor, may this does "nothing"...)
4. Rebuild & up containers

After that the error disappeared, but seems podman is not working fine with fallback variables (e,g, `${VARIABLE:-fallbackValue`) anymore. I had something like that in my `compose.yaml`, which is passed to my `Caddyfile`:
```yaml
environment:
    SERVER_ADDRESSES: :80 ${SERVER_NAME:-localhost}
```

Passing `SERVER_NAME` with `.env` file helped as workaround.

I've also re-enabled apparmor & rebooted the system afterwards, seems "all" is working fine again :-)

### Update²

The mount flag does "nothing", seems it's enough to run `podman system prune -a` & re-build containers after upgrade (except the variable fallback issue).

Note that cleanup is generally performed in a process running the background with stderr/out attached so you it is possible you don't see the errors.

For debugging you should ensure that if all containers are stopped the rootless-netns pasta process is gone as well. And of course since this is an apparmor problem check the kernel audit log.


z/Z is only for selinux relabelling so not something you need on ubuntu.
compose it not directly support by podman, you either use podman-compose or docker-compose so if you think there is a problem with parsing env vars you must report it to the respective repo.
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
I am going to close this, this does not seem like a upstream problem but rather the distro level security policies that did not allow what we need
